summary: Send integer body fields when explicitly set to 0 (gate on cmd.Flags().Changed instead of != 0), and require --name on sequences create.
reason: The generated create/update commands omitted any int flag whose value was 0, so documented, valid values were unreachable: sequences send_hour=0 (midnight) and sequence-email delay_value=0 (the only way to make an immediate first email) could never be transmitted. Also filled the empty stdin-guard block on sequences create with the required --name validation the API enforces, so a missing name returns a clear error instead of a cryptic 422.
validated_outcome: sequences create --name X --send-hour 0 --dry-run now emits "send_hour": 0 in the request body; sequences create with no --name returns "--name is required" instead of sending an empty body.
